Cogon-Mt. Naupa-Balirong Trail Loop | Cogon / Balirong, Naga | 2026 GPX
Home > Trails > Naga

Cogon-Mt. Naupa-Balirong Trail Loop | Cogon / Balirong, Naga | 2026 GPX

The Cogon–Mt. Naupa–Balirong Trail Loop is an 11 km loop hike in Naga, Cebu. The route combines community trails, rural roads, and open countryside as it passes through the Mt. Naupa area before descending toward Balirong and returning to Cogon. The documented hike took place in July 2026 under dry trail conditions.

Trail Snapshot

MILD
View others →
📅 DocumentedJuly 2026
📏 Distance11 km (Loop)
⛰ Elevation Gain421 m
🕒 Duration3.5 - 4 hrs
💧 WaterAt least 2 liters
🌳 ShadeLimited
👣 Surface30% trail, 70% road
🗺️ GPXSupporter Access

Access

The documented hike began near Cogon Chapel, where motorcycle parking was available without a parking fee. Road access to the trailhead was via a concrete road. Public transportation was limited during this visit, while habal-habal services and private vehicle access were available.

Why this trail is rated Mild

✓ Mostly clear trail with occasional navigation awareness required
✓ Moderate hiking distance
✓ Sustained but manageable climbs or descents
✓ Some isolated sections between communities
✓ Assistance may require additional time to reach

On this trail: farmland • rolling hills • viewpoint

Route Description

The route follows a combination of rolling community trails, rural roads, and open countryside, with continuous landscape views for much of the hike. Short climbs and descents alternate throughout the loop, creating a steady cardiovascular workout without long sustained ascents or descents. Houses and local activity are present along much of the route, so it rarely feels isolated, although a few quieter sections require additional navigation awareness where the trail becomes faint.

After leaving the Mt. Naupa area, parts of the original route had been cultivated, making it take some time to locate the next trail segment. A descent into the valley toward Balirong also includes a faint trail, suggesting relatively few people use that section. Near the Balirong exit, the route crosses a simple river before continuing toward Cogon Road, which forms the final climb back to Cogon Chapel. This final section is a steady uphill road with very few flat sections before reaching the starting point.

Photo Album
Trail Photos Every photo captured along the trail.
↗

Trail Conditions

The trail was dry during the documented hike, with a cloudy morning providing the day’s weather. Approximately 30% of the route followed trails, while the remaining 70% used roads. Shade was limited, and carrying at least 2 liters of water was recommended.

Mobile signal remained strong throughout most of the hike, with only a few dead spots. Stores were available near communities along the route. Insect activity was high, and the hike encountered mostly local residents, with a few hikers near the Mt. Naupa summit.

Additional Notes

The routes were generally well-trodden and regularly used by both locals and livestock. Although some sections felt isolated, motorcycles from nearby roads could still be heard.

Several narrow concrete road improvement projects were present along the route, although work appeared to be stalled during the documented hike, with no machinery observed. It was mango season, resulting in many fallen and rotting mangoes beneath plantations. Some roadside sections also passed pig farms, making those areas noticeably smelly.

Cows were present in the area but did not block the trail during this hike. Friendly conversations took place with several local residents, including children and adults, who were described as helpful and welcoming.
